1	"""Local execution environment with interrupt support and non-blocking I/O."""
2	
3	import glob
4	import os
5	import platform
6	import shutil
7	import signal
8	import subprocess
9	import threading
10	import time
11	
12	_IS_WINDOWS = platform.system() == "Windows"
13	
14	from tools.environments.base import BaseEnvironment
15	from tools.environments.persistent_shell import PersistentShellMixin
16	from tools.interrupt import is_interrupted
17	
18	# Unique marker to isolate real command output from shell init/exit noise.
19	# printf (no trailing newline) keeps the boundaries clean for splitting.
20	_OUTPUT_FENCE = "__HERMES_FENCE_a9f7b3__"
21	
22	# Hermes-internal env vars that should NOT leak into terminal subprocesses.
23	# These are loaded from ~/.hermes/.env for Hermes' own LLM/provider calls
24	# but can break external CLIs (e.g. codex) that also honor them.
25	# See: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/1002
26	#
27	# Built dynamically from the provider registry so new providers are
28	# automatically covered without manual blocklist maintenance.
29	_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX = "_HERMES_FORCE_"
30	
31	
32	def _build_provider_env_blocklist() -> frozenset:
33	    """Derive the blocklist from provider, tool, and gateway config.
34	
35	    Automatically picks up api_key_env_vars and base_url_env_var from
36	    every registered provider, plus tool/messaging env vars from the
37	    optional config registry, so new Hermes-managed secrets are blocked
38	    in subprocesses without having to maintain multiple static lists.
39	    """
40	    blocked: set[str] = set()
41	
42	    try:
43	        from hermes_cli.auth import PROVIDER_REGISTRY
44	        for pconfig in PROVIDER_REGISTRY.values():
45	            blocked.update(pconfig.api_key_env_vars)
46	            if pconfig.base_url_env_var:
47	                blocked.add(pconfig.base_url_env_var)
48	    except ImportError:
49	        pass
50	
51	    try:
52	        from hermes_cli.config import OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS
53	        for name, metadata in OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS.items():
54	            category = metadata.get("category")
55	            if category in {"tool", "messaging"}:
56	                blocked.add(name)
57	            elif category == "setting" and metadata.get("password"):
58	                blocked.add(name)
59	    except ImportError:
60	        pass
61	
62	    # Vars not covered above but still Hermes-internal / conflict-prone.
63	    blocked.update({
64	        "OPENAI_BASE_URL",
65	        "OPENAI_API_KEY",
66	        "OPENAI_API_BASE",         # legacy alias
67	        "OPENAI_ORG_ID",
68	        "OPENAI_ORGANIZATION",
69	        "OPENROUTER_API_KEY",
70	        "ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL",
71	        "ANTHROPIC_TOKEN",         # OAuth token (not in registry as env var)
72	        "CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN",
73	        "LLM_MODEL",
74	        # Expanded isolation for other major providers (Issue #1002)
75	        "GOOGLE_API_KEY",          # Gemini / Google AI Studio
76	        "DEEPSEEK_API_KEY",        # DeepSeek
77	        "MISTRAL_API_KEY",         # Mistral AI
78	        "GROQ_API_KEY",            # Groq
79	        "TOGETHER_API_KEY",        # Together AI
80	        "PERPLEXITY_API_KEY",      # Perplexity
81	        "COHERE_API_KEY",          # Cohere
82	        "FIREWORKS_API_KEY",       # Fireworks AI
83	        "XAI_API_KEY",             # xAI (Grok)
84	        "HELICONE_API_KEY",        # LLM Observability proxy
85	        "PARALLEL_API_KEY",
86	        "FIRECRAWL_API_KEY",
87	        "FIRECRAWL_API_URL",
88	        # Gateway/runtime config not represented in OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS.
89	        "TELEGRAM_HOME_CHANNEL",
90	        "TELEGRAM_HOME_CHANNEL_NAME",
91	        "DISCORD_HOME_CHANNEL",
92	        "DISCORD_HOME_CHANNEL_NAME",
93	        "DISCORD_REQUIRE_MENTION",
94	        "DISCORD_FREE_RESPONSE_CHANNELS",
95	        "DISCORD_AUTO_THREAD",
96	        "SLACK_HOME_CHANNEL",
97	        "SLACK_HOME_CHANNEL_NAME",
98	        "SLACK_ALLOWED_USERS",
99	        "WHATSAPP_ENABLED",
100	        "WHATSAPP_MODE",
101	        "WHATSAPP_ALLOWED_USERS",
102	        "SIGNAL_HTTP_URL",
103	        "SIGNAL_ACCOUNT",
104	        "SIGNAL_ALLOWED_USERS",
105	        "SIGNAL_GROUP_ALLOWED_USERS",
106	        "SIGNAL_HOME_CHANNEL",
107	        "SIGNAL_HOME_CHANNEL_NAME",
108	        "SIGNAL_IGNORE_STORIES",
109	        "HASS_TOKEN",
110	        "HASS_URL",
111	        "EMAIL_ADDRESS",
112	        "EMAIL_PASSWORD",
113	        "EMAIL_IMAP_HOST",
114	        "EMAIL_SMTP_HOST",
115	        "EMAIL_HOME_ADDRESS",
116	        "EMAIL_HOME_ADDRESS_NAME",
117	        "GATEWAY_ALLOWED_USERS",
118	        # Skills Hub / GitHub app auth paths and aliases.
119	        "GH_TOKEN",
120	        "GITHUB_APP_ID",
121	        "GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY_PATH",
122	        "GITHUB_APP_INSTALLATION_ID",
123	        # Remote sandbox backend credentials.
124	        "MODAL_TOKEN_ID",
125	        "MODAL_TOKEN_SECRET",
126	        "DAYTONA_API_KEY",
127	    })
128	    return frozenset(blocked)
129	
130	
131	_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_BLOCKLIST = _build_provider_env_blocklist()
132	
133	
134	def _sanitize_subprocess_env(base_env: dict | None, extra_env: dict | None = None) -> dict:
135	    """Filter Hermes-managed secrets from a subprocess environment.
136	
137	    `_HERMES_FORCE_<VAR>` entries in ``extra_env`` opt a blocked variable back in
138	    intentionally for callers that truly need it.  Vars registered via
139	    :mod:`tools.env_passthrough` (skill-declared or user-configured) also
140	    bypass the blocklist.
141	    """
142	    try:
143	        from tools.env_passthrough import is_env_passthrough as _is_passthrough
144	    except Exception:
145	        _is_passthrough = lambda _: False  # noqa: E731
146	
147	    sanitized: dict[str, str] = {}
148	
149	    for key, value in (base_env or {}).items():
150	        if key.startswith(_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX):
151	            continue
152	        if key not in _HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_BLOCKLIST or _is_passthrough(key):
153	            sanitized[key] = value
154	
155	    for key, value in (extra_env or {}).items():
156	        if key.startswith(_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX):
157	            real_key = key[len(_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX):]
158	            sanitized[real_key] = value
159	        elif key not in _HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_BLOCKLIST or _is_passthrough(key):
160	            sanitized[key] = value
161	
162	    return sanitized
163	
164	
165	def _find_bash() -> str:
166	    """Find bash for command execution.
167	
168	    The fence wrapper uses bash syntax (semicolons, $?, printf), so we
169	    must use bash — not the user's $SHELL which could be fish/zsh/etc.
170	    On Windows: uses Git Bash (bundled with Git for Windows).
171	    """
172	    if not _IS_WINDOWS:
173	        return (
174	            shutil.which("bash")
175	            or ("/usr/bin/bash" if os.path.isfile("/usr/bin/bash") else None)
176	            or ("/bin/bash" if os.path.isfile("/bin/bash") else None)
177	            or os.environ.get("SHELL")  # last resort: whatever they have
178	            or "/bin/sh"
179	        )
180	
181	    # Windows: look for Git Bash (installed with Git for Windows).
182	    # Allow override via env var (same pattern as Claude Code).
183	    custom = os.environ.get("HERMES_GIT_BASH_PATH")
184	    if custom and os.path.isfile(custom):
185	        return custom
186	
187	    # shutil.which finds bash.exe if Git\bin is on PATH
188	    found = shutil.which("bash")
189	    if found:
190	        return found
191	
192	    # Check common Git for Windows install locations
193	    for candidate in (
194	        os.path.join(os.environ.get("ProgramFiles", r"C:\Program Files"), "Git", "bin", "bash.exe"),
195	        os.path.join(os.environ.get("ProgramFiles(x86)", r"C:\Program Files (x86)"), "Git", "bin", "bash.exe"),
196	        os.path.join(os.environ.get("LOCALAPPDATA", ""), "Programs", "Git", "bin", "bash.exe"),
197	    ):
198	        if candidate and os.path.isfile(candidate):
199	            return candidate
200	
201	    raise RuntimeError(
202	        "Git Bash not found. Hermes Agent requires Git for Windows on Windows.\n"
203	        "Install it from: https://git-scm.com/download/win\n"
204	        "Or set HERMES_GIT_BASH_PATH to your bash.exe location."
205	    )
206	
207	
208	# Backward compat — process_registry.py imports this name
209	_find_shell = _find_bash
210	
211	
212	# Noise lines emitted by interactive shells when stdin is not a terminal.
213	# Used as a fallback when output fence markers are missing.
214	_SHELL_NOISE_SUBSTRINGS = (
215	    # bash
216	    "bash: cannot set terminal process group",
217	    "bash: no job control in this shell",
218	    "no job control in this shell",
219	    "cannot set terminal process group",
220	    "tcsetattr: Inappropriate ioctl for device",
221	    # zsh / oh-my-zsh / macOS terminal session
222	    "Restored session:",
223	    "Saving session...",
224	    "Last login:",
225	    "command not found:",
226	    "Oh My Zsh",
227	    "compinit:",
228	)
229	
230	
231	def _clean_shell_noise(output: str) -> str:
232	    """Strip shell startup/exit warnings that leak when using -i without a TTY.
233	
234	    Removes lines matching known noise patterns from both the beginning
235	    and end of the output.  Lines in the middle are left untouched.
236	    """
237	
238	    def _is_noise(line: str) -> bool:
239	        return any(noise in line for noise in _SHELL_NOISE_SUBSTRINGS)
240	
241	    lines = output.split("\n")
242	
243	    # Strip leading noise
244	    while lines and _is_noise(lines[0]):
245	        lines.pop(0)
246	
247	    # Strip trailing noise (walk backwards, skip empty lines from split)
248	    end = len(lines) - 1
249	    while end >= 0 and (not lines[end] or _is_noise(lines[end])):
250	        end -= 1

file.content:
"""Local execution environment with interrupt support and non-blocking I/O."""

import glob
import os
import platform
import shutil
import signal
import subprocess
import threading
import time

_IS_WINDOWS = platform.system() == "Windows"

from tools.environments.base import BaseEnvironment
from tools.environments.persistent_shell import PersistentShellMixin
from tools.interrupt import is_interrupted

# Unique marker to isolate real command output from shell init/exit noise.
# printf (no trailing newline) keeps the boundaries clean for splitting.
_OUTPUT_FENCE = "__HERMES_FENCE_a9f7b3__"

# Hermes-internal env vars that should NOT leak into terminal subprocesses.
# These are loaded from ~/.hermes/.env for Hermes' own LLM/provider calls
# but can break external CLIs (e.g. codex) that also honor them.
# See: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/1002
#
# Built dynamically from the provider registry so new providers are
# automatically covered without manual blocklist maintenance.
_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX = "_HERMES_FORCE_"


def _build_provider_env_blocklist() -> frozenset:
    """Derive the blocklist from provider, tool, and gateway config.

    Automatically picks up api_key_env_vars and base_url_env_var from
    every registered provider, plus tool/messaging env vars from the
    optional config registry, so new Hermes-managed secrets are blocked
    in subprocesses without having to maintain multiple static lists.
    """
    blocked: set[str] = set()

    try:
        from hermes_cli.auth import PROVIDER_REGISTRY
        for pconfig in PROVIDER_REGISTRY.values():
            blocked.update(pconfig.api_key_env_vars)
            if pconfig.base_url_env_var:
                blocked.add(pconfig.base_url_env_var)
    except ImportError:
        pass

    try:
        from hermes_cli.config import OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS
        for name, metadata in OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS.items():
            category = metadata.get("category")
            if category in {"tool", "messaging"}:
                blocked.add(name)
            elif category == "setting" and metadata.get("password"):
                blocked.add(name)
    except ImportError:
        pass

    # Vars not covered above but still Hermes-internal / conflict-prone.
    blocked.update({
        "OPENAI_BASE_URL",
        "OPENAI_API_KEY",
        "OPENAI_API_BASE",         # legacy alias
        "OPENAI_ORG_ID",
        "OPENAI_ORGANIZATION",
        "OPENROUTER_API_KEY",
        "ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL",
        "ANTHROPIC_TOKEN",         # OAuth token (not in registry as env var)
        "CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN",
        "LLM_MODEL",
        # Expanded isolation for other major providers (Issue #1002)
        "GOOGLE_API_KEY",          # Gemini / Google AI Studio
        "DEEPSEEK_API_KEY",        # DeepSeek
        "MISTRAL_API_KEY",         # Mistral AI
        "GROQ_API_KEY",            # Groq
        "TOGETHER_API_KEY",        # Together AI
        "PERPLEXITY_API_KEY",      # Perplexity
        "COHERE_API_KEY",          # Cohere
        "FIREWORKS_API_KEY",       # Fireworks AI
        "XAI_API_KEY",             # xAI (Grok)
        "HELICONE_API_KEY",        # LLM Observability proxy
        "PARALLEL_API_KEY",
        "FIRECRAWL_API_KEY",
        "FIRECRAWL_API_URL",
        # Gateway/runtime config not represented in OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS.
        "TELEGRAM_HOME_CHANNEL",
        "TELEGRAM_HOME_CHANNEL_NAME",
        "DISCORD_HOME_CHANNEL",
        "DISCORD_HOME_CHANNEL_NAME",
        "DISCORD_REQUIRE_MENTION",
        "DISCORD_FREE_RESPONSE_CHANNELS",
        "DISCORD_AUTO_THREAD",
        "SLACK_HOME_CHANNEL",
        "SLACK_HOME_CHANNEL_NAME",
        "SLACK_ALLOWED_USERS",
        "WHATSAPP_ENABLED",
        "WHATSAPP_MODE",
        "WHATSAPP_ALLOWED_USERS",
        "SIGNAL_HTTP_URL",
        "SIGNAL_ACCOUNT",
        "SIGNAL_ALLOWED_USERS",
        "SIGNAL_GROUP_ALLOWED_USERS",
        "SIGNAL_HOME_CHANNEL",
        "SIGNAL_HOME_CHANNEL_NAME",
        "SIGNAL_IGNORE_STORIES",
        "HASS_TOKEN",
        "HASS_URL",
        "EMAIL_ADDRESS",
        "EMAIL_PASSWORD",
        "EMAIL_IMAP_HOST",
        "EMAIL_SMTP_HOST",
        "EMAIL_HOME_ADDRESS",
        "EMAIL_HOME_ADDRESS_NAME",
        "GATEWAY_ALLOWED_USERS",
        # Skills Hub / GitHub app auth paths and aliases.
        "GH_TOKEN",
        "GITHUB_APP_ID",
        "GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY_PATH",
        "GITHUB_APP_INSTALLATION_ID",
        # Remote sandbox backend credentials.
        "MODAL_TOKEN_ID",
        "MODAL_TOKEN_SECRET",
        "DAYTONA_API_KEY",
    })
    return frozenset(blocked)


_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_BLOCKLIST = _build_provider_env_blocklist()


def _sanitize_subprocess_env(base_env: dict | None, extra_env: dict | None = None) -> dict:
    """Filter Hermes-managed secrets from a subprocess environment.

    `_HERMES_FORCE_<VAR>` entries in ``extra_env`` opt a blocked variable back in
    intentionally for callers that truly need it.  Vars registered via
    :mod:`tools.env_passthrough` (skill-declared or user-configured) also
    bypass the blocklist.
    """
    try:
        from tools.env_passthrough import is_env_passthrough as _is_passthrough
    except Exception:
        _is_passthrough = lambda _: False  # noqa: E731

    sanitized: dict[str, str] = {}

    for key, value in (base_env or {}).items():
        if key.startswith(_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX):
            continue
        if key not in _HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_BLOCKLIST or _is_passthrough(key):
            sanitized[key] = value

    for key, value in (extra_env or {}).items():
        if key.startswith(_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX):
            real_key = key[len(_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX):]
            sanitized[real_key] = value
        elif key not in _HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_BLOCKLIST or _is_passthrough(key):
            sanitized[key] = value

    return sanitized


def _find_bash() -> str:
    """Find bash for command execution.

    The fence wrapper uses bash syntax (semicolons, $?, printf), so we
    must use bash — not the user's $SHELL which could be fish/zsh/etc.
    On Windows: uses Git Bash (bundled with Git for Windows).
    """
    if not _IS_WINDOWS:
        return (
            shutil.which("bash")
            or ("/usr/bin/bash" if os.path.isfile("/usr/bin/bash") else None)
            or ("/bin/bash" if os.path.isfile("/bin/bash") else None)
            or os.environ.get("SHELL")  # last resort: whatever they have
            or "/bin/sh"
        )

    # Windows: look for Git Bash (installed with Git for Windows).
    # Allow override via env var (same pattern as Claude Code).
    custom = os.environ.get("HERMES_GIT_BASH_PATH")
    if custom and os.path.isfile(custom):
        return custom

    # shutil.which finds bash.exe if Git\bin is on PATH
    found = shutil.which("bash")
    if found:
        return found

    # Check common Git for Windows install locations
    for candidate in (
        os.path.join(os.environ.get("ProgramFiles", r"C:\Program Files"), "Git", "bin", "bash.exe"),
        os.path.join(os.environ.get("ProgramFiles(x86)", r"C:\Program Files (x86)"), "Git", "bin", "bash.exe"),
        os.path.join(os.environ.get("LOCALAPPDATA", ""), "Programs", "Git", "bin", "bash.exe"),
    ):
        if candidate and os.path.isfile(candidate):
            return candidate

    raise RuntimeError(
        "Git Bash not found. Hermes Agent requires Git for Windows on Windows.\n"
        "Install it from: https://git-scm.com/download/win\n"
        "Or set HERMES_GIT_BASH_PATH to your bash.exe location."
    )


# Backward compat — process_registry.py imports this name
_find_shell = _find_bash


# Noise lines emitted by interactive shells when stdin is not a terminal.
# Used as a fallback when output fence markers are missing.
_SHELL_NOISE_SUBSTRINGS = (
    # bash
    "bash: cannot set terminal process group",
    "bash: no job control in this shell",
    "no job control in this shell",
    "cannot set terminal process group",
    "tcsetattr: Inappropriate ioctl for device",
    # zsh / oh-my-zsh / macOS terminal session
    "Restored session:",
    "Saving session...",
    "Last login:",
    "command not found:",
    "Oh My Zsh",
    "compinit:",
)


def _clean_shell_noise(output: str) -> str:
    """Strip shell startup/exit warnings that leak when using -i without a TTY.

    Removes lines matching known noise patterns from both the beginning
    and end of the output.  Lines in the middle are left untouched.
    """

    def _is_noise(line: str) -> bool:
        return any(noise in line for noise in _SHELL_NOISE_SUBSTRINGS)

    lines = output.split("\n")

    # Strip leading noise
    while lines and _is_noise(lines[0]):
        lines.pop(0)

    # Strip trailing noise (walk backwards, skip empty lines from split)
    end = len(lines) - 1
    while end >= 0 and (not lines[end] or _is_noise(lines[end])):
        end -= 1
